howItWorks.intro: This bulking calorie calculator uses standard formulas to provide accurate results.
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates. Consult a professional for critical decisions.

faq.question: How many extra calories for bulking?
faq.answer: A lean bulk adds 10-15% above maintenance (250-500 cal/day). This supports muscle growth while minimizing fat gain. Aggressive bulking (500+) gains muscle faster but with more fat.

faq.question: How much protein for muscle gain?
faq.answer: 1.0g per pound of body weight is the standard recommendation for maximizing muscle protein synthesis during a bulk.

faq.question: How fast should I gain weight?
faq.answer: 0.5-1 lb per week for beginners, 0.25-0.5 for intermediates. Faster gain means more fat. If gaining over 1 lb/week, you are likely adding unnecessary fat.

faq.question: How long should I bulk?
faq.answer: Until body fat reaches 15-18% (men) or 25-28% (women), then transition to a cut. Typical bulk lasts 3-6 months.
